stem: A website uses authentication to check the identity of its users before they can access their account.
model_answer: Authentication confirms that a user is who they claim to be. The most common method is a username and password: the user provides a username, the system hashes the password they type and compares it with the hash stored for that account. Only if they match is the user allowed in. Stronger authentication adds a second factor (e.g. a code sent to the user's phone) so that a stolen password on its own is not enough.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe what authentication is and how a username/password system typically works.

mark_points.text: authentication checks/confirms that the user is who they claim to be
user supplies a username and password
password is checked (e.g. hashed and compared) against the stored value; match = access granted, mismatch = denied
accepted_alternatives: system compares hash of typed password with stored hash
if passwords match user is allowed in
